amendments.normalized_body: The round-trip struct-drift tests (`start_journal_details_round_trips_with_model_render` and `completion_journal_details_round_trips_with_model_render`) populate all optional fields with `Some` values, which correctly catches missing fields via the re-render equality assertion. However, neither `StartJournalDetails` nor `CompletionJournalDetails` uses `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]`. This means that in production (outside the test), if a new field is added to the model's render function but not to the fs adapter's parsing struct, parsing will silently succeed and `merge()` will silently discard the unrecognized field — the exact failure mode Issue 1 describes. The round-trip test catches this in CI, but adding `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]` to both `StartJournalDetails` and `CompletionJournalDetails` would also catch it at runtime, providing defense-in-depth. If forward-compatibility with newer journal formats is a concern, this could be gated behind a test-only assertion instead.

`plan_hash` auto-population is still incomplete for the repair/replay paths. In `src/adapters/fs.rs:2907-2918`, replaying an existing running row only backfills the caller-supplied `plan_hash` and explicitly skips any snapshot fallback. In `src/adapters/fs.rs:3215-3248`, terminal replays/finalizations do the same. The result is that any pre-fix lineage row already stored with `plan_hash: None` stays unlabeled forever when start/completion is replayed with `plan_hash=None`, even in the safe case where the milestone snapshot already had the canonical hash before this attempt started. That leaves lineage incomplete on the exact recovery path this task is trying to fix. Add a safe snapshot-backed repair path for existing rows as well, and cover it with a test that finalizes a legacy `plan_hash=None` row after replay under an unchanged snapshot.

However, neither `StartJournalDetails` nor `CompletionJournalDetails` uses `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]`. This means that in production (outside the test), if a new field is added to the model's render function but not to the fs adapter's parsing struct, parsing will silently succeed and `merge()` will silently discard the unrecognized field — the exact failure mode Issue 1 describes. The round-trip test catches this in CI, but adding `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]` to both `StartJournalDetails` and `CompletionJournalDetails` would also catch it at runtime, providing defense-in-depth. If forward-compatibility with newer journal formats is a concern, this could be gated behind a test-only assertion instead.

payload.summary: Final review accepted 1 amendment(s); restart required.
